description: First controlled PvP farm-ladder (2026-07-04, WOS_BattleReports_LadderCalibration_v9.pdf). Attacker 沃军 (T10 inf + T6 marks, Bradley only hero) vs defender [RTS]Colonel Muller (T7 inf + T6 marks). NO-PROC design (T6 marks, no lancers => deterministic - CONFIRMED by 3 identical 6Kv6K repeats). Both sides 50-50 inf/marks. All attacker VICTORY, defender fully wiped.
design_note: SYMMETRIC scaling - both sides' counts grew together (4Kv3K, 6Kv6K, 10Kv10K), NOT fixed-attacker/varying-defender. A symmetric NxN ladder is self-similar (constant loss fraction: 6v6 and 10v10 BOTH give 19.3% attacker-inf loss) so it CANNOT isolate the defender frontage exponent def_ed. Still valuable: confirms determinism + scale-invariance + is a clean 2-sided PvP calibration anchor.
determinism: 6Kv6K run 3x (19:47:20, 19:50:25, 19:51:03) -> identical (att injured 203, survivors 5,421). No-proc PvP is deterministic.

battles.note: 4K vs 3K - attacker OUTNUMBERS -> lower loss fraction

key_findings: Attacker MARKS untouched every battle (protected behind the inf wall); all attacker casualties are INFANTRY.
SYMMETRIC battles are SCALE-INVARIANT: 6v6 and 10v10 both -> 19.3% attacker-inf loss. Battle A (4v3, attacker outnumbers) -> 12.2%.
att 'kills' column = defender SEVERE bucket exactly (1050/2100/3500 = injured); severe:light ~35:65 (0.35 severe share, matches fortress convention).
Bradley S3 procs 3/4/4 -> ~12/16/16 turns; duration grows then ~plateaus - coarse (x4 turns resolution).
next: REDO as FIXED-ATTACKER: same attacker army EVERY battle (large enough to win vs 16K defender), vary ONLY defender 3K/6K/10K/16K. Attacker-inf casualties vs defender count then reveal def_ed (linear=1 vs frontage-limited~0.48).
